articles.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Pushes Crude Prices Higher
articles.expand: June WTI crude oil (CLM26 ) on Monday closed up +2.65 (+2.78%), and June RBOB gasoline (RBM26 ) closed up +0.0731 (+2.07%). Crude oil and gasoline prices settled sharply higher on Monday after President Trump rejected Iran's response to his latest peace proposal, which prolongs the closure of the Strait...

articles.title: US Dollar: Hotter CPI and equity risks – ING
articles.expand: ING strategists Francesco Pesole, Frantisek Taborsky and Chris Turner expect a 0.9% MoM United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI) print, above consensus, with core at 0.3% MoM.

articles.title: German ZEW Sentiment Rises to -10.2, But Economy Still Burdened by Energy Shock
articles.expand: Germany’s ZEW Economic Sentiment index improved from -17.2 to -10.2 in May, beating expectations of -20.5 and signaling a modest improvement in investor confidence despite ongoing economic pressures linked to the Middle East conflict. Eurozone ZEW Economic Sentiment also rose sharply from -20.4 to -9.1, while the Eurozone current conditions gauge i...

articles.title: EUR/GBP Surges as Markets Price “Zombie Government” Risk as Starmer Crisis Deepens
articles.expand: Sterling plunged sharply today while UK bond yields surged above 5.1% after the first ministerial resignation calling for Prime Minister Keir Starmer to step down transformed simmering political anxiety into something much more dangerous for markets: the perception that Britain may now be drifting toward a “zombie government.” The trigger came shor...

articles.title: Japanese Yen underperforms vs USD as Iran tensions offset intervention risks before US CPI
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair reverses a knee-jerk decline to the 156.75-156.70 region and climbs to the top end of its daily range during the first half of the European session.

articles.title: ECB: June hike call challenged by data path – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities economists diverge from consensus by expecting the European Central Bank (ECB) to hold rates in June. They stress the absence of clear second-round effects, anchored inflation expectations, and softening confidence and growth.

articles.title: Dow Jones futures remains flat ahead of US CPI inflation data
articles.expand: Dow Jones futures are steady around 49,800 during the European hours on Tuesday, ahead of the United States (US) regular opening. Meanwhile, the S P 500 decline 0.35% to near 7,410, and the Nasdaq 100 futures inch lower 0.77% toward 29,200.

articles.title: Gold remains depressed near $4,700 on firmer USD; looks to US CPI for fresh impetus
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) maintains its offered tone below through the early European session on Tuesday and is currently placed around the $4,700 as traders keenly await the release of the US consumer inflation figures.

articles.title: Australian Dollar: Budget tightening focus – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank's Senior Market Strategist Benjamin Picton highlights comments from Australian Prime Minister Albanese and Treasurer Chalmers ahead of the Commonwealth budget, stressing concerns over an expensive housing market and planned tax changes.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Languishes near 0.7200 as USD sticks to gains ahead of US CPI
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair continues with its struggle to find acceptance above the 0.7250 level and attracts fresh sellers on Tuesday as rising US-Iran tensions boost the safe-haven US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Euro: Bearish-leaning against as ZEW stays weak – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole notes EUR/USD has held up with risk sentiment despite Gulf tensions, but warns current levels may not withstand an equity correction.

articles.title: ECB’s Nagel: Baseline includes two rate hikes
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council Member and President of the Deutsche Bundesbank, Joachim Nagel, said during the European trading session on Tuesday that the baseline includes two interest rate hikes in the near term.

articles.title: US CPI data set to highlight inflation accelerated in April as Oil prices remain high
articles.expand: The US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will publish the April Consumer Price Index (CPI) data on Tuesday.

articles.title: ECB policymaker Nagel says data will determine ECB's decision in June
articles.expand: Our mandate requires us to act if inflation expectations de-anchor Data will determine ECB's decision in June Baseline included two rate hikes The ECB has already signalled that a rate hike in June is coming unless the war ends and oil prices fall significantly before then, so I'm not sure why he places weight on the data. The economic data hasn't ...

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Prepares for fresh rally above 1.3700
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair trades 0.12% higher to near 1.3695 during the European trading session on Tuesday.

articles.title: Fed: Rate cuts contingent on Strait of Hormuz – BNY
articles.expand: BNY strategists John Velis and David Tam argue that resilient United States (US) data and likely elevated inflation prints make it harder to justify Federal Reserve (Fed) rate cuts this year.

articles.title: The overall risk mood leans more defensively as we get into European trading today
articles.expand: The US-Iran conflict continues to drag on and at this stage, it looks like nothing will change for at least another week. After keeping somewhat steadier yesterday, markets are starting to run into a bit of nerves today as we see a more defensive risk posture take shape. Oil prices are climbing back up with WTI crude now up near 2% on the day and t...

articles.title: NZDJPY Flashback Update: Preparing For Another Wave Higher
articles.expand: NZDJPY remains below the 95 resistance zone and could still be forming a wave four correction before another push higher toward 96–97.

articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, the only highlight is the German ZEW index. The data is expected to show the third contraction in a row due to the situation in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz closure. The market-reaction will likely be muted given that it won't change anything for the ECB. AMERICAN SESSION In the American session...

articles.title: Euro strengthens against British Pound on UK political uncertainty, ECB hawkish signals
articles.expand: The EUR/GBP cross gains traction near 0.8665 during the early European session on Tuesday. The British Pound (GBP) weakens against the Euro (EUR) amid political instability in the United Kingdom (UK). Traders brace for the ZEW surveys from Germany and the Eurozone, which are due later on Tuesday.

articles.title: US Dollar Index: Supported by yields before CPI – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank analysts note the US Dollar Index (DXY) inched up to 97.96 as markets consolidated and prepared for United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI).

articles.title: Germany headline inflation nudges up on higher energy prices due to Middle East conflict

That comes as no surprise with it being part of the fallout from the US-Iran conflict. Of note, overall energy ...

articles.title: FX Watch: EUR/USD and USD/JPY Setups to Watch if U.S. CPI Comes in Hot
articles.expand: If Uncle Sam drops a much hotter inflation picture, USD traders may want to check EUR/USD and USD/JPY charts for opportunities.

articles.title: Alphabet plans first yen bond sale to fund $190 billion AI spending push
articles.expand: Alphabet is planning its first yen-denominated bond sale across up to eight maturities, expected to total several hundred billion yen, as part of a broader push to fund $190 billion in AI capital spending this year. Summary: Alphabet is planning its first yen-denominated bond sale in a multi-tranche offering spanning maturities of three, five, seve...

articles.expand: Bank of Japan’s Summary of Opinions from the April meeting revealed a noticeably more hawkish debate inside the board, with several policymakers arguing that another interest rate hike could come sooner rather than later as the Iran war intensifies inflation pressures. articles.title: FX Watch: USD/CHF and AUD/USD Triangle Breakouts If U.S. CPI Fails to Impress
articles.expand: Markets are expecting a significantly upbeat U.S. CPI to tip the scales in favor of Fed tightening soon, but what if the numbers fail to impress? Here’s what I’m watching on USD/CHF and AUD/USD in this scenario.

articles.title: Australian business confidence remains deeply pessimistic, -24 in April (prior -29)
articles.expand: Australian business confidence edged up to -24 in April from -29 in March but remained deeply negative, as surging energy costs squeezed margins and conditions fell to their lowest since 2020, NAB data showed. Summary: NAB's business confidence index improved marginally to -24 in April from -29 in March, remaining deeply negative after March's seco...

articles.title: BOJ 'Summary" - Japan rate hike back on table as BOJ signals next move still likely upward
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan held rates at its April meeting but warned upside inflation risks are rising as crude oil prices surge, with several members flagging possible hikes as soon as the next meeting. Summary: The Bank of Japan kept its policy interest rate unchanged at its April 27-28 meeting, citing the need for a wait-and-see approach given the uncer...

articles.title: UK consumer spending falls for first time since 2024 as Iran war bites
articles.expand: UK consumer spending fell 0.1% in April, the first annual decline since late 2024, as Iran war fears drove households toward savings and away from big-ticket and travel spending, Barclays and BRC data showed. Summary: Barclays credit and debit card spending fell 0.1% year-on-year in April, reversing a 0.9% gain in March, the first decline since Nov...

articles.title: Global Dairy Markets Shift Focus to Protein Demand Amid Surging Supply
articles.expand: Global dairy markets are experiencing a structural shift as milk supply expands and butterfat values weaken. New Zealand's record milk flows are contributing to aggressive WMP exports, while whey remains supply-constrained. The GDT event shows a powder-led recovery despite potential risks from China’s FMD outbreak.

articles.title: Most hospitals in north, western Ontario have budget deficits: Report
articles.expand: More than half of Ontario hospitals are running budget deficits, and the issue is worse in some areas of the province, a new report says

articles.title: Geopolitical tension now seen as top economic risk in Bank of Canada survey
articles.expand: Geopolitical tensions are now considered a bigger risk to Canada’s economic productivity than trade conflict with countries like the U.S, according to the Bank of Canada‘s latest survey data. articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.

articles.expand: The USDCHF continues to lean against its 100-hour moving average as a key resistance barometer. Corrective rallies last week stalled against that moving average on Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday — and today’s rally attempt once again failed near the same level. articles.title: Weekly Forex Forecast – AUD/USD, S&P 500 Index, NASDAQ 100 Index, Brent Crude Oil, Gasoline
articles.expand: As the prospects of a peace deal between the USA and Iran seemed to rise again, risk assets and stocks rose last week, while energy prices took a hit. Find out if a turnaround might happen this week as we face another Trump war deadline due Thursday.

articles.title: AUD/USD Signal: Aussie Fades Early Gains, Buy the Dip at 0.71
articles.expand: The Australian dollar gives back early Thursday gains with resistance at 0.7250, but RBA rate hikes support a buy-the-dip strategy targeting a return to highs with key support holding at 0.71.

articles.title: USD/CHF Forecast: Dollar Drifts Below 0.7775 as Yields Drop, Rebound Eyed Above 0.78
articles.expand: The US dollar slips below 0.7775 vs the Swiss franc as 10-year yields fall on Middle East peace hopes, with traders eyeing a potential rebound above 0.78 ahead of Friday's NFP release.
